title: Mammoth Configuration

name.description: Human-readable Mammoth instance name used in logs, status output, metrics, and future dashboards. Keep stable per environment.

mammoth.description: Mammoth instance identity and operator-facing metadata.

node_id.description: Stable node identifier used by status output and future control-plane agents.

fleet_id.description: Optional fleet identifier for grouping Mammoth nodes.

environment.description: Optional deployment environment such as development, staging, or production.

node.description: Optional local node identity. Mammoth OSS uses this for status and future extension surfaces only.

host.description: PostgreSQL host reachable from the Mammoth process or pod.

username.description: PostgreSQL user used by Mammoth. This user must be able to connect in replication mode and read publication metadata.

password_env.description: Name of the environment variable containing the PostgreSQL password. The password itself should not be stored in YAML.

postgres.description: PostgreSQL connection settings for the source database.

slot.description: PostgreSQL logical replication slot name. Permanent slots preserve WAL until Mammoth acknowledges progress. Changing this value creates a separate replication position.

publications.description: PostgreSQL publications to subscribe to. Publications define which table changes PostgreSQL sends to Mammoth.

start_lsn.description: Optional Log Sequence Number for advanced recovery or controlled replay. Leave null or unset for normal checkpoint-based operation.

auto_create_slot.description: When true, Mammoth creates the replication slot if it does not exist. Convenient for development; DBA-managed slots are recommended for production.

temporary_slot.description: When true, use a temporary slot that disappears when the replication session ends. Durable deployments should use false.

feedback_interval.description: Standby feedback interval in seconds. Lower values improve liveness/progress visibility; higher values reduce chatter.

replication.description: Logical replication settings. Mammoth uses one replication slot and one WAL stream; downstream runtime concurrency does not create additional replication connections.

name.description: Destination name used in logs, dead-letter records, and future metrics.

url.description: Destination URL for delivered CDC payloads. Transaction delivery sends a TransactionEnvelope payload; event delivery sends a single event payload.

timeout_seconds.description: HTTP timeout in seconds for webhook delivery attempts.

headers.description: Static HTTP headers added to every webhook request. Do not put secrets here; use header_env for secret-backed values.

header_env.description: HTTP headers whose values are read from environment variables. Keys are header names; values are environment variable names.

algorithm.description: Webhook signing algorithm. Mammoth signs '<timestamp>.<body>' using HMAC-SHA256.

secret_env.description: Environment variable containing the webhook signing secret.

signature_header.description: Header name used for the generated signature. Defaults to X-Mammoth-Signature.

timestamp_header.description: Header name used for the signing timestamp. Defaults to X-Mammoth-Timestamp.

signing.description: Optional HMAC-SHA256 request signing for webhook receivers.

webhook.description: HTTP webhook destination settings. This is shorthand for a single webhook destination; use destinations for fanout.

name.description: Destination name used in delivered ledgers, dead-letter records, logs, and metrics.

type.description: Destination adapter type. Mammoth OSS 0.7.x supports webhook destinations.

enabled.description: When false, Mammoth skips this destination, advances local progress for the work item, and does not attempt delivery.

url.description: Webhook URL for delivered CDC payloads. Transaction delivery sends a TransactionEnvelope payload; event delivery sends a single event payload.

timeout_seconds.description: HTTP timeout in seconds for this destination's delivery attempts.

headers.description: Static HTTP headers added to every request for this destination. Do not put secrets here; use header_env for secret-backed values.

header_env.description: HTTP headers whose values are read from environment variables. Keys are header names; values are environment variable names.

algorithm.description: Webhook signing algorithm. Mammoth signs '<timestamp>.<body>' using HMAC-SHA256.

secret_env.description: Environment variable containing the webhook signing secret.

signature_header.description: Header name used for the generated signature. Defaults to X-Mammoth-Signature.

timestamp_header.description: Header name used for the signing timestamp. Defaults to X-Mammoth-Timestamp.

signing.description: Optional HMAC-SHA256 request signing for this webhook destination.

schemas.description: PostgreSQL schemas this destination should receive. Omit to match every schema.

tables.description: PostgreSQL tables this destination should receive. Omit to match every table.

operations.description: CDC operations this destination should receive, such as insert, update, or delete. Omit to match every operation.

route.description: Optional destination route filter. Event delivery matches one event; transaction delivery matches when any event in the transaction matches and delivers the full envelope.

max_attempts.description: Destination-specific maximum delivery attempts before dead-lettering. Defaults to the top-level retry.max_attempts.

schedule_seconds.description: Destination-specific retry backoff schedule in seconds. Defaults to the top-level retry.schedule_seconds.

retry.description: Optional retry policy override for this destination.

destinations.description: Fanout destination list. Each CDC work item is delivered independently to each configured destination.

max_attempts.description: Maximum delivery attempts before moving the event or envelope to the dead-letter store.

schedule_seconds.description: Progressive retry delay schedule in seconds. If attempts exceed the schedule length, the final value may be reused.

retry.description: Retry policy for failed delivery attempts before dead-lettering.

path.description: Filesystem path to Mammoth SQLite operational database. In Kubernetes, back this path with persistent storage.

sqlite.description: SQLite operational store settings for checkpoints, attempts, and dead-letter records.

adapter.description: Operational state adapter. Mammoth OSS 0.7.x ships the sqlite adapter.

operational_state.description: Operational state adapter selection. SQLite remains the default OSS adapter.

level.description: Log verbosity. Use debug for diagnostics, info for normal operation, and warn/error for quieter production output.

unit.description: Delivery granularity. event delivers each change independently; transaction delivers a complete TransactionEnvelope and is the safer default for checkpoint correctness.

scope.description: Ordering scope. global preserves complete WAL order; transaction preserves commit order; relation preserves per-table order; primary_key preserves per-row order; none is best-effort only.

ordering.description: Ordering policy consumed by the delivery runtime.

adapter.description: Delivery runtime adapter. inline executes synchronously; concurrent uses CDC::Concurrent::ProcessorPool for I/O-heavy delivery work.

concurrency.description: Number of downstream delivery workers. Does not create extra PostgreSQL replication slots or connections.

batch_size.description: Number of work units submitted to the concurrent runtime together. Values greater than 1 allow ordering-insensitive deliveries to overlap more visibly.

preserve_order.description: When true, preserve configured delivery ordering while using the runtime. Disable only for idempotent ordering-insensitive destinations.

timeout_seconds.description: Optional runtime-level timeout in seconds. Leave null to rely on destination-specific timeouts.

runtime.description: Downstream delivery runtime settings. Runtime concurrency affects webhook/destination delivery only, not PostgreSQL replication stream count.

host.description: Bind host for the optional Mammoth observability HTTP server exposing /healthz, /readyz, and /metrics.

port.description: TCP port for the optional Mammoth observability HTTP server.

observability.description: Optional health, readiness, and metrics endpoint settings.

description: Mammoth relay configuration. Mammoth consumes PostgreSQL logical replication changes and delivers them to downstream destinations while preserving checkpoint, retry, and dead-letter semantics.
